perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that (i) the documents provided to the Receiving Party hereunder containing Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party shall be used by the Receiving Party solely for the purpose of evaluating its interest in the business arrangements described or performing a future agreement between the Parties; (ii) it will not use such documents disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Proprietary Information to anyone except its
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party understands that (i) the documents provided to the Receiving Party hereunder containing Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party should be used by the Receiving Party solely for the purpose of evaluating its interest in the business arrangements described or performing a future agreement between the Parties; (ii) it will not use such documents disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Proprietary Information to anyone except its
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees' to 'understands' weakens the obligation. Also the use of 'shall' is replaced with 'should'. This creates a contradiction because 'agrees' implies a binding promise, whereas 'understands' merely acknowledges a concept. Similarly 'shall' enforces the legal contract but 'should' just suggests a course of action. It is implied that documents may now be used for more than just evaluating business interests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands that (i) the documents provided to the Receiving Party hereunder containing Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party should be used by the Receiving Party solely for the purpose of evaluating its interest in the business arrangements described or performing a future agreement between the Parties; (ii) it will not use such documents disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Proprietary Information to anyone except its 1 <PAGE> employees with a need to know who are involved in the consideration or performance of the business arrangements described herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'agrees' to 'understands' and 'shall' to 'should' weakens the obligation on the Receiving Party. The contradicted text restates this weakened obligation. However, the initial intention of the original text was that the Receiving Party was legally obligated to not use such documents disclosed for any other purpose, and now the changed text opens up the possibility that they may use the documents for other purposes without violating the contract. This undermines the original intention and creates a contradiction because it now implies that the Receiving Party might not be legally bound to the constraints stated in the original text. This is in direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Each party may disclose any Proprietary Information to the extent that such Party has been advised by counsel that such disclosure is necessary to comply with laws or regulations, or any judicial order or request, or as necessary in connection with the filing of any patent, copyright, or similar application or registration; provided that each Party shall give the other Party reasonable advance notice of such proposed disclosure, shall where practicable use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment of any such Proprietary Information and shall advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party should disclose any Proprietary Information to the extent that such Party has been advised by counsel that such disclosure is necessary to comply with laws or regulations, or any judicial order or request, or as necessary in connection with the filing of any patent, copyright, or similar application or registration; provided that each Party must give the other Party reasonable advance notice of such proposed disclosure, must where practicable use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment of any such Proprietary Information and will advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'may disclose' to 'should disclose' and 'shall give' to 'must give' creates conflicting degrees of obligation. One suggests a recommendation to disclose and the other is enforcing that something has to be given. This creates an internal contradiction on how they must approach this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not apply to Information that: (a) is in or enters the public domain, through no fault of the Receiving Party; or (b) is or has been disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the other Party or to a third party without restriction; or (c) is already in the possession of the Receiving Party, without restriction and prior to disclosure of the Information hereunder; or (d) is or has been lawfully disclosed by a third party to the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality; or (e) is developed by the Receiving Party independently of any breach of this Agreement; or (f) the applicable period of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3 has ended. Each party should disclose any Proprietary Information to the extent that such Party has been advised by counsel that such disclosure is necessary to comply with laws or regulations, or any judicial order or request, or as necessary in connection with the filing of any patent, copyright, or similar application or registration; provided that each Party must give the other Party reasonable advance notice of such proposed disclosure, must where practicable use its best efforts to secure confidential treatment of any such Proprietary Information and will advise the other Party in writing of the manner of the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'may disclose' indicating permission, while the changed text uses 'should disclose', suggesting a recommendation, and 'shall give' which is a requirement, changed to 'must give' which is also a requirement. The contradictory text shows that 'This Agreement shall not apply to Information that: (b) is or has been disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the other Party or to a third party without restriction'. If parties are obliged to disclose, then they would be disclosing without restriction which is not what is expected under the agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Thus, the contradictory text shows that disclosure is optional, while the changed text makes it closer to an obligation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate two (2) years from the date first written above, except the obligations of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3, and the terms of paragraph 4, shall continue for the period specified in paragraph 3.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may terminate two (2) years from the date first written above, except the obligations of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3, and the terms of paragraph 4, should continue for the period specified in paragraph 3.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall terminate' to 'may terminate' and 'shall continue' to 'should continue' creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration and ongoing obligations. This suggests the agreement might not automatically end, which contradict the set terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may terminate two (2) years from the date first written above, except the obligations of confidentiality pursuant to paragraph 3, and the terms of paragraph 4, should continue for the period specified in paragraph 3.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall terminate' and 'shall continue,' indicating a mandatory action and obligation. The changed text modifies these to 'may terminate' and 'should continue,' introducing uncertainty and optionality. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, thus the initial change causes the contradiction. The change from mandatory to optional directly conflicts with the need for clear and enforceable terms regarding the agreement's duration and ongoing obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction by making the contract's enforcement unclear, leading to ambiguity as to whether or not the contract will terminate after 2 years and whether the terms of paragraph 4 should continue.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall have, or shall enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants that will safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party shall advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party should have, or may enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants that will safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party will advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall have' to 'should have' and 'shall enter into' to 'may enter into' reduces the mandatory nature of safeguarding agreements. This introduces uncertainty in terms of safeguarding of information, while enforcing through 'will advise' that employees have to be warned about their obligations, which contradicts the need for safeguarding agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party should have, or may enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions, subsidiary companies and consultants that will safeguard the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to employees, the Receiving Party will advise all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information as to their obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall have' to 'should have' and 'shall enter into' to 'may enter into' weakens the mandatory nature of safeguarding agreements. This directly conflicts with the statement that employees 'will advise' all employees who will have access to Proprietary Information, creating an in-text contradiction because the mandatory warning to the employees contradicts the weakening of the requirement for safeguarding agreements with parent companies, divisions, subsidiaries, and consultants.
